Continue into the spacious dining room, ideal for hosting large gatherings with friends and family. Just steps away, the bright living room offers a lovely view of the front yard and works perfectly as a formal sitting area or entertainment space. As you move through the first floor, you’ll find a cozy family room featuring a gas fireplace, creating the perfect setting for relaxing winter evenings. Convenient main-level laundry and interior access to the garage are located just around the corner, adding everyday practicality. Head up the custom staircase to the second floor where you’ll find four generously sized bedrooms and two full bathrooms. The first three bedrooms are versatile and well-sized—perfect for kids, guests, or a home office. The hall bathroom offers a bathtub and plenty of space for daily use by family and visitors. The primary bedroom is a true retreat, complete with hardwood flooring, a large walk-in closet, and a beautifully updated en-suite bathroom with a modern feel. Make your way down to the basement, which is partially finished and offers flexible space for a rec room, home gym, office, or playroom, plus excellent storage options to keep everything organized. Last but certainly not least, step outside to your backyard oasis. A large deck overlooks the expansive yard and pool, making it the perfect spot for morning coffee, summer barbecues, and outdoor entertaining. The yard provides plenty of room for play, pets, and gardening, while the pool is ready for hot summer days and weekend fun. The shed can be used for additional storage or enjoyed in its current setup as a bar area—an ideal hangout to watch sporting events or host friends. Major systems offer peace of mind: the roof, furnace, and air conditioning system are approximately 10 years old, and the AC features a brand-new compressor to keep the home cool and comfortable all summer long. With generous space, a fantastic backyard, a convenient location, and tasteful updates throughout, this home is an excellent opportunity that is not expected to last. Schedule your tour today!

PublicRemarks: Exceptional Private Estate with Resort-Style Amenities & Professional-Grade Kitchen Welcome to a truly one-of-a-kind property built in 1792, which offers the perfect blend of luxury living, modern amenities, and outdoor lifestyle while still preserving hints of its historical charm. This extraordinary estate, situated on a beautifully maintained, farm-assessed 6.29-acre parcel, offers both everyday comfort and unforgettable entertaining. At the heart of the home is an impressive 24’ x 40’ chef’s kitchen, thoughtfully designed for culinary enthusiasts and professionals alike. This space features top-of-the-line commercial-grade appliances, including a True 3-glass-door refrigerator, Viking 8-burner double oven with grill, Gaggenau vegetable steamer and deep fryer, wine cooler, ice maker, beer mister, and gas grill. Granite countertops, tile flooring, two dishwashers, five sinks, and a pot filler complete this exceptional workspace. Dual ventilation systems and both forced hot air and a wood-burning stove ensure year-round comfort. The home offers 4 bedrooms and 3.5 baths, including two full baths on the second level, a full bath in the basement, and a convenient first-floor powder room. Additional highlights include two fireplaces, dual staircases to the second floor, and a full basement with finished areas ideal for a gym, recreation space, or additional storage. The kitchen and adjacent den are equipped with central air, while the main home is heated by hot water baseboard. Step outside to experience an incredible outdoor retreat. A 3,800 sq. ft. elevated composite deck serves as the centerpiece, complete with a fully enclosed, insulated gazebo featuring heat, A/C, carpeting, and dual ventilation systems. A second courtyard gazebo offers underground electric, water, and built-in speakers—perfect for entertaining. Nature lovers will appreciate the 2,000-gallon koi pond with dual waterfalls, mature landscaping with evergreen and deciduous trees, a productive chestnut tree, and a thriving orchard with apple, pear, and peach trees. A fenced 40’ x 60’ raised bed garden and professionally installed chicken coops with underground utilities provide the perfect setup for hobby farming or sustainable living. Additional exterior features include a new heated above-ground pool, a lighted basketball court, and underground water lines with winterization capability. The property also benefits from a gravity septic system, a 300-ft deep well, a whole-house generator with an underground propane tank, and a full water purification system. For those needing space and versatility, the property includes a large barn with open and enclosed storage areas, as well as a heated workshop equipped with an oil furnace, wood stove, large sliding doors, and 200-amp electrical service. The main home also offers 200-amp service, and the barn is separately metered. This remarkable estate offers a rare opportunity to enjoy luxury, privacy, and functionality—all in one exceptional setting. Select equipment, including a front-end loader and zero-turn mower, may be available separately.

Don't pass up this rare opportunity to own a WATERFRONT 2 bedroom, 2 1/2 bath END unit Townhouse in Burlington Citys best kept secret......Quaker Landing! Besides the spectacular river views from every side and back window and door there's no grass to cut, no snow to shovel, no exterior maintenance, beautiful interiors and manicured exteriors leaving you with time to relax on your patio and watch the boats float by. Sound like your kind of place???  Then welcome to #9 Quaker Landing, a 2 story brick townhouse literally a stones throw from the Delaware River.  Assigned parking along with your own detached garage leaves you just a few steps from your front door.  The first floor of this unique home is an entertainers delight with an open floor plan huge 25 x 13 living room, dining area, eat in kitchen and powder room. The living room/dining area boasts a gas remote control fireplace with mantle and slate heart, crown moulding and hardwood flooring along with a double swingset French door that leads to the back patio and of course....the river views! The updated eat in kitchen features oak designer cabinetry with light rail and under counter lighting, crema granite countertops, peninsula/island, ceramic floor and backsplash and eat at breakfast counter! All major appliances shown(plus a washer and dryer) are included. An oak scissor staircase takes you to the second floor which features a 15 x 15 primary bedroom, a dressing area with walk in closet and makeup/desk sink and a primary bath. The bath features a dbl sink, full length mirror, neo shower and a full size soaking tub plus.......you can see the river from all 3 rooms! A second bedroom with views of the front with its own full bath and walk in closet along with a laundry closet between the two bedrooms completes the second floor. Outside theres a private walkway that takes you from the front to back yard, including a greenhouse and trellis on your way to and from the rear patio and river walk. If you are not familiar with the area, Quaker Landing is a semi secluded development of just a few townhouses built in the mid 1980's along the Delaware River featuring an HOA to take care of all the exterior needs. It is its own little community slightly off the beaten path but not far from everything including the Burlington Bristol Bridge, The Riverline and the Rt 130 shopping corridor.  Please note that this property requires flood insurance and is being sold "as-is"(Seller will not make any repairs).

PublicRemarks: Grandeur with everyday livability, soaring heights and privacy abound. This exquisite home sits on a wooded cul de sac on 2.2 acres in Medford and delivers on that feeling of something special. With its grand entry height, the hardwood floors and visual pull of your eye with the staircase leading up this home draws you in. To the right of the foyer is a classically appointed office with French doors that open to richly finished, floor to ceiling cabinetry and shelving creating much more than a “home office” feel. To the left of the foyer is the home’s more formal living room, with a coffered ceiling that adds architectural detail while the richly finished plantation shutters deliver a contrast and warmth to the room. The living room opens to the formal dining room offering room for a generously proportioned table and ready for large gatherings. It is, however, the back of the home that is truly the heart and soul and quite frankly, the showstopper of spaces. The kitchen is an entertainer’s dream space and calling it a kitchen doesn’t seem to do it justice as it is expansive, detailed and open. It opens to and overlooks a grand sunroom, that features a vaulted ceiling, with walls of windows and views that bring the outdoors in and is flooded in light. The kitchen also extends and opens to the family room which features a soaring 2-story ceiling height and is anchored with a fireplace adding yet another visual detail and seasonal coziness. The kitchen is large; however, it is not “cold” – it has grace and character and feels like home. It is the heartbeat of the home and offers warm and contrasting tones with cabinetry in an off-white and an island in a darker finish that offers seating and corbels of carved wood. The wet bar and beverage area complement the room and offer a club feel. The details, the scope and feel of this living space need to be experienced, to be taken in and fully appreciated. The patio is an extension of the home, it is the seasonal complement with a stone and paver design that follows the back of the home and includes a built-in grill and stone fireplace. A water feature/koi pond adds to the tranquility of the outdoor setting, where pines create a calming swoosh with each breeze and where the world melts away. Back inside the home, the second floor presents three generously proportioned bedrooms that share an updated full hall bath. The primary suite offers separation from the other bedrooms and offers calming space. The primary ensuite is wonderfully appointed with a corner soaking tub, an oversized vanity with plenty of countertop space and storage drawers along with two sink areas. Beyond all these wonderful rooms is a finished basement. And like the rest of the home, details even in the finished basement were not forgotten. Set up for movie, family fun and entertaining, the basement features a screening area and a kitchen for snacks and fun. A full bath and a possible guest room, office or home gym space is ready and waiting. This is a stately home, grand and detailed yet designed to be lived in, enjoyed and not fussed over; it is home.

PublicRemarks: A landmark Moorestown Victorian, restored and restructured to reveal its architectural character while introducing a more open and livable design. Three-story living with ten-foot ceilings on the main level, original moldings, grand pocket doors, and a handcrafted staircase anchor the home’s character. Structural reconfiguration creates brighter interiors with improved flow throughout, including a finished lower level. The main level is introduced through a foyer that reads as a room of its own, where restored inlaid wood floors, original millwork, and a handcrafted staircase establish the architectural tone carried throughout the home. The layout was restructured to create uninterrupted sightlines, allowing each room to connect while maintaining a clear sense of scale and purpose. The family room functions as a more continuous living space, with preserved fireplace detail that anchors the room. A private study with custom millwork and built-ins offers a more focused setting. A newly constructed dining room addition extends from the kitchen, scaled for both everyday use and entertaining. The kitchen features handcrafted inset cabinetry in a soft ivory finish with polished nickel hardware and a 10-foot walnut island. Bianco Rhino marble extends across countertops and a full-height backsplash, complemented by 18 x 18 marble tile flooring. Appliances include a 48” Wolf range, integrated Sub-Zero refrigerator and freezer, and a panel-ready dishwasher, maintaining a clean, furniture-like aesthetic. A butler’s pantry continues the material palette with inset cabinetry and marble surfaces, supporting the adjacent dining room. A private primary wing is accessed through newly added double doors. A balcony, accessible from both the bedroom and bath, extends the suite outward and brings natural light into the space. The spa bath includes custom cabinetry, Rohl fixtures, a steam shower with seating, and marble mosaic detailing. Secondary baths are finished with Newport Brass fixtures. Lighting selections include fixtures by Hudson Valley, Visual Comfort, and Currey & Company. A second-floor laundry room is located on the bedroom level, supporting everyday function. A private suite positioned above the garage introduces additional flexibility, designed to function as a second primary, guest quarters, or an expansive recreation space. Measuring approximately 25' x 14'6", the room is complemented by a full bath and two custom-outfitted walk-in closets (each approximately 5'10" x 5'3"). The scale and layout also lend themselves to a shared suite configuration. Originally conceived as two bedrooms, the space was intentionally left open to allow future owners to tailor it to their needs. The third level offers two additional bedrooms, a full bath, and a large bonus room suited for media, fitness, or workspace, along with walk-in attic storage. The finished lower level provides additional flexible living space, a powder room, and newly poured concrete flooring in portions of the level. Additional improvements include a mudroom with garage entry, new roof, Andersen Energy Star-rated windows, updated mechanical systems, three-zone HVAC, two tankless water heaters, upgraded electrical service, and Lutron Caseta smart lighting. Exterior updates include a new asphalt driveway, bluestone walkways, and landscape improvements. Originally built in 1886, this home has been restored and restructured to preserve its architectural integrity while offering a more functional and refined living experience. Located approximately 2 miles from Laurel Creek Country Club, 0.5 miles from Moorestown Friends School, and 1.9 miles from I-295, with Center City Philadelphia approximately 12 miles away.

Competitively priced compared to other current listings in the development, this home offers real value for buyers looking for move-in condition, generous living space, and an unbeatable location. Lovingly maintained and in amazing condition, this Colonial townhouse offers approximately 1,716 sq ft above grade plus a surprisingly spacious lower level, giving you three levels of living space and the kind of storage that is incredibly hard to find in condo living. While some finishes reflect a more classic style, the home has been beautifully cared for and offers a wonderful opportunity to personalize over time with simple cosmetic touches. The kitchen is truly one of the standout features of the home. Completely remodeled in 2017, it was thoughtfully designed for real everyday living with abundant beautiful wood cabinets, solid stone countertops, all new flooring, a dedicated coffee bar area, and a stunning floor-to-ceiling pantry cabinet with custom pull-out doors that maximizes both storage and convenience. The kitchen is generously sized with room to add a trendy mobile island for even more prep and gathering space, making it ideal for one cook or even two. During the renovation, the wall between the kitchen and dining/living area was opened with a peekaboo window and seated bar, creating a more connected and open feel that buyers love for both daily living and entertaining. Natural light pours into the home, and the townhome feels quiet, peaceful, and tucked away. From the living room, step right out onto the oversized private wood deck with a beautiful view of the park, creating a perfect extension of the living space for relaxing or entertaining. The lower level offers rare flexibility and exceptional storage with five separate rooms and areas, including a finished space currently used as a gym, a huge walk-in pantry/storage area, a laundry room, a utility room, and an additional storage room. If storage has been on your wish list, this home delivers in a way that is very hard to find in condo living. The second floor features an extra-large primary bedroom with beautiful natural light from the backyard side, along with a dressing area, two side-by-side closets, and a private primary bath with a walk-in shower. The two additional bedrooms are both nicely sized, and an oversized hallway closet provides even more practical storage. Moorestown Mews is a wonderfully small community of just 65 condos, known for its friendly neighbors, peaceful setting, and long-term residents, and homes here are rarely available. From your front door, you can stroll right out to Main Street to enjoy Moorestown’s restaurants, shopping, and beloved annual parades and festivals. Additional convenience includes one assigned parking spot right out front for easy access. This is a special opportunity to own a spacious, light-filled home in one of Moorestown’s most cherished condo communities, with an unbeatable location, an outstanding remodeled kitchen, and a layout that offers comfort, flexibility, and lasting value. Note: hot water heater 12/2020, HVAC 2013, and roof approximately 6 years old.

Work in the city and come home to your beautiful retreat to unwind! Welcome to 45 Bordentown Crosswicks Rd, an exquisite Italianate Victorian home built in 1840, nestled on 2.13 private acres in Chesterfield Township. With over 4,800 sq ft of interior living space across three stories, this remarkable residence blends timeless historic charm with thoughtful modern updates. Step inside and be greeted by the grandeur of 11-foot ceilings, original hardwood floors, crown molding and historic details such as the stamped-tin ceiling, stained glass windows and the original banister and railings. The updated eat-in kitchen is both functional and beautiful, featuring granite countertops, white shaker cabinets, stainless steel appliances, a farmhouse sink, subway tile backsplash, and a brick wood-burning faux fireplace. Bench seating and built-in storage lockers create a cozy yet practical space, while French doors lead out to the backyard oasis. The second floor offers 4 bedrooms and 2 full bathrooms including a spacious primary suite with a walk-in closet with custom built-ins and a spa-like bath with tiled flooring and an original clawfoot tub. A convenient second-floor laundry room and a flexible space perfect for an office or sitting area complete this level. On the third floor, you’ll find 5 additional bedrooms and a full bath featuring a huge tiled shower with glass doors, a soaking tub and marble top vanity—ideal for large households or guests. Outside, enjoy resort-style living with a heated Gunite saltwater pool with hot tub (fits up to 15 people), surrounded by stamped concrete and lush custom landscaping with mature trees. The property also includes a detached garage (with one bay converted into a game room), new garage doors, a 40 x 65 pole barn currently set up as a gym, batting cage and a baseball training station!  Additional features include a 7 year old roof on the main home,  radiator heat, a tankless water heater and a serene screened in sunroom that offers the perfect space to relax and take in the surrounding beauty. Don’t miss this rare opportunity to own a piece of history that seamlessly combines elegance, space, and modern comfort in the heart of Crosswicks.

PublicRemarks: This beautifully restored Moorestown treasure offers more than 7,000 square feet of character that simply can’t be replicated today. Step through the doors and you’re instantly transported— this 19th-century carriage house, once part of the famed Strawbridge Estate and dated c.1890 by the Historical Society, carries its history with quiet pride. Set on a peaceful cul-de-sac in the heart of town, this 7,140 sq. ft., two-and-a-half-story home features 5 bedrooms and 4.5 baths, all thoughtfully renewed in 2015. The owners worked hand- in-hand with an architect, builder, and structural engineer to bring the property up to modern standards without sacrificing the soul of the original structure. The result is a seamless blend of old-world craftsmanship and updated comfort. A one-of-a-kind front gate, hand-built from the estate’s original mahogany horse stalls, sets the tone. Inside, a spacious double foyer welcomes you. Off to the left, a cheerful three-season room flows onto a sweeping covered porch that runs the full length of the home. The ground level, once the carriage bay, now offers a spectacular 33' x 37' family room crowned with a tin ceiling and illuminated by a vintage ship’s-wheel light fixture. Off this impressive space, four original horse stalls remain intact, complete with preserved ironwork and millwork. Pocket doors allow you to showcase them or keep them tucked away—either way, it’s an unforgettable feature. Beyond the stalls is a quiet hideaway lined with original woodwork, leading to a second kitchen, full bath, historic dumbwaiter, and bedroom. With its own private entrance, this area is perfect for guests or multigenerational living. Head upstairs via the main staircase, decorated with recessed art niches, or take the hidden elevator. On the second floor, once home to the hay loft, you’ll find a welcoming landing, a large laundry room with half bath, and two charming en-suite bedrooms. The primary suite includes an entry chamber ideal for a cozy reading nook, office, or nursery. The second suite stands out with built-ins, wainscoting, and a warm plank ceiling. The main kitchen offers a refreshed galley layout with granite counters, farmhouse sink, subway tile, ample cabinetry, and a walk-in pantry. It opens into a dramatic two-story living and dining area featuring original hardwood floors, beamed ceiling, a wood-burning fireplace, and newly added windows that flood the space with natural light. From here, step into the inviting sunroom and out onto a large deck overlooking the fenced backyard, perfect for gatherings. Separate staircases connect the living spaces to the first level. Continue to the third floor to find a cozy overlook and a double bedroom with cedar closets and a full bath highlighted by a reclaimed 20th-century wood door. It’s an ideal space for kids, guests, or a whimsical retreat. A spacious connected garage and a generous driveway provide ample parking. Close to parks, shops, and dining, this is truly a home that must be experienced in person to appreciate. Rare, storied, and lovingly revived, this is your chance to own a remarkable piece of Moorestown history.

PublicRemarks: Step into timeless elegance at this extraordinary Moorestown estate, where Great Gatsby-level glamour meets modern comfort and functionality. Originally built in 1924 and gracefully situated on just over an acre in the heart of town, this stately residence is more than just a home—it’s a legacy. With walkable access to Main Street, top-tier schools, and easy proximity to Philadelphia and the shore points, it offers a rare blend of prestige and convenience. Unlike many homes of its era, which leaned into Art Deco or Neo-Gothic styles, this residence draws from the more refined Federal style. Its classic architecture—double-hung windows, covered entry, and symmetry—exudes grace, while the scale and layout feel warm, welcoming, and entirely livable for today's lifestyle. From the moment you step into the grand center hall, you're met with impeccable craftsmanship: floor-to-ceiling windows topped with fanlights, layered custom millwork, and original detailing that whispers of the home’s rich heritage. The traditional living and dining rooms provide formal entertaining space, while a cozy screened-in porch invites easy conversation and quiet morning coffees. For the modern entertainer and culinary enthusiast, the kitchen wing is a dream. Anchored by a massive island and hand-painted Italian-tile pizza oven, this space also features a walk-in pantry, catering kitchen, and beautiful wood floors throughout the butler’s pantry. A thoughtful addition by a previous owner expanded this area to include a casual dining space, den, drop zone, and even a fitness room above—plus a full basement below with soaring ceilings. Throughout the home, you'll find worldly touches—from imported French mantels and lighting to the Andalusian-style gathering room with a custom bar and terracotta floors overlooking the pool. A rich mahogany library with coffered ceilings and fireplace is perfect for work or winding down. Downstairs, the finished basement offers surprises at every turn: a wine tasting room, craft space, theater stage, full kitchen, billiards area, and more—ideal for both creative escape and large-scale entertaining. Upstairs, the primary suite is a private retreat with dual bathrooms, custom closets, and a tucked-away office space. Three additional bedroom suites grace the second floor, with even more flexible rooms on the third—perfect for guests, teens, or staff quarters. Outside, the resort-style backyard is built to impress: a sparkling pool and spa, a full outdoor kitchen with multiple grills and warming drawers, a wood-burning fireplace, and a striking bluestone patio with brick inlay. The restored three-level barn has been converted into a two-car garage and awaits your personal finishing touches—think studio, office, or guest suite. This one-of-a-kind home offers the rare combination of historical significance, architectural artistry, luxurious living, and a location that truly has it all. Schedule your private tour and experience what it feels like to live in a home that’s as rich in story as it is in style.

PublicRemarks: WE ARE SOLD OUT OF HOME SITES --  Welcome to East Gate at Wexford, Bruce Paparone's single-family new home community in the vibrant town of Lumberton, Burlington County, New Jersey.   This new home community is comprised of 92 single family homesites laid out over a beautiful landscape, offering a variety of lots, from quiet cul-de-sacs to secluded backyards sloping toward open space.   At East Gate at Wexford, Paparone New Homes offers four signature models with a 5-year tax abatement.  Floorplans range from 2,028-3,230+ square feet and come in a choice of architectural styles.  Paparone is committed to building a unique home for every buyer, with a wide selection of designer finishes, fixtures, and structural options.  Located along Route 38, this prime location is just minutes from Route I-295 and the NJ Turnpike, a convenient commute to both South and Central Jersey. It’s surrounded by premier shopping and dining destinations in Moorestown, Mt. Laurel and Medford. Errands are easy with Wegmans, Target, Lowe's, and Walmart just a short drive away. And get ready for a great summer with beach towns like Long Beach Island and Asbury Park only an hour away!  Along with the convenience to major highways, East Gate at Wexford falls within the highly ranked Lumberton School District and Rancocas Valley High School.  The township municipal complex is less than 10 minutes down the road and boasts fields for every sport, a park, and wooded hiking paths.
